---
title: "MLB Discrete Outcome Model"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Model Prep

```{r}
library(baseballr)
library(tidyverse)
library(tidymodels)
library(zoo)

raw_schedule <- mlb_schedule(season = 2025, level_ids = 1)

# Clean and turn it into your mlb_training_data
mlb_training_data <- raw_schedule %>%
  # Filter for regular season games that are officially completed
  filter(game_type == "R", status_abstract_game_state == "Final") %>%
  mutate(
    # Extract runs scored
    home_runs = as.numeric(teams_home_score),
    away_runs = as.numeric(teams_away_score),
    
    # Create the binary target variable (Win vs Loss) for the home team
    home_team_win = if_else(home_runs > away_runs, "Win", "Loss"),
    home_team_win = as.factor(home_team_win),
    
    total_game_runs = home_runs + away_runs
  ) %>%
  # Clean up column names to keep it simple
  select(home_team_win, total_game_runs, 
         home_team = teams_home_team_name, 
         away_team = teams_away_team_name, 
         home_runs, away_runs)


```

```{r}

team_stats <- mlb_training_data %>%
  group_by(home_team) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_home_runs_scored  = mean(home_runs, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_home_runs_allowed = mean(away_runs, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 2. Join these season baselines back to your match data
predictive_mlb_data <- mlb_training_data %>%
  left_join(team_stats, by = "home_team") %>%
  # Filter out any NA joins if they exist
  filter(!is.na(avg_home_runs_scored))

# 3. Re-split the dataset using your new predictive features
set.seed(42)
data_split <- initial_split(predictive_mlb_data, prop = 0.80, strata = home_team_win)
train_data <- training(data_split)
test_data  <- testing(data_split)

# 4. Specify and fit the model using baseline averages instead of live final scores
log_reg_spec <- logistic_reg() %>% 
  set_engine("glm") %>% 
  set_mode("classification")

predictive_model <- log_reg_spec %>% 
  fit(home_team_win ~ avg_home_runs_scored + avg_home_runs_allowed, data = train_data)

# 5. Look at your new statistical summary (the warnings should be gone!)
print(tidy(predictive_model))

# 6. Generate predictions and explicitly tell roc_auc which level is the "Win"
predictions <- predict(predictive_model, new_data = test_data, type = "prob") %>%
  bind_cols(predict(predictive_model, new_data = test_data, type = "class")) %>%
  bind_cols(test_data)

# Specify options = list(event_level = "second") so R knows 'Win' is the target event
roc_auc_score <- predictions %>% 
  roc_auc(truth = home_team_win, .pred_Win, event_level = "second")

print(roc_auc_score)
```

## Model 2 Prep

```{r}
home_stats <- mlb_training_data %>%
  group_by(home_team) %>%
  summarise(
    home_avg_scored  = mean(home_runs, na.rm = TRUE),
    home_avg_allowed = mean(away_runs, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

away_stats <- mlb_training_data %>%
  group_by(away_team) %>%
  summarise(
    away_avg_scored  = mean(away_runs, na.rm = TRUE),
    away_avg_allowed = mean(home_runs, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Combine baselines into a single predictive dataset for linear regression
linear_mlb_data <- mlb_training_data %>%
  left_join(home_stats, by = "home_team") %>%
  left_join(away_stats, by = "away_team") %>%
  filter(!is.na(home_avg_scored), !is.na(away_avg_scored))


## RMSE Root mean squared error and R^2
set.seed(42)
lin_split <- initial_split(linear_mlb_data, prop = 0.80)
lin_train <- training(lin_split)
lin_test  <- testing(lin_split)

# Specify the Linear Regression Model
lin_spec <- linear_reg() %>%
  set_engine("lm") %>%
  set_mode("regression")

# Fit the model to predict total game runs
run_model <- lin_spec %>%
  fit(total_game_runs ~ home_avg_scored + home_avg_allowed + away_avg_scored + away_avg_allowed,
      data = lin_train)

# Check model metrics on test data
lin_predictions <- predict(run_model, new_data = lin_test) %>%
  bind_cols(lin_test)

metrics_summary <- lin_predictions %>%
  metrics(truth = total_game_runs, estimate = .pred)
```

TODAY'S MATCHUPS

```{r}
todays_games_full <- raw_today %>%
  filter(date == "2026-06-24", game_type == "R") %>%
  select(home_team = teams_home_team_name, away_team = teams_away_team_name) %>%
  # Join Model 1 requirements (exact column names it was trained on)
  left_join(home_stats %>% rename(avg_home_runs_scored = home_avg_scored, 
                                  avg_home_runs_allowed = home_avg_allowed), 
            by = "home_team") %>%
  # Join Model 2 requirements (keeping home_avg versions for Model 2)
  left_join(home_stats, by = "home_team") %>%
  left_join(away_stats, by = "away_team") %>%
  filter(!is.na(home_avg_scored), !is.na(away_avg_scored))


# 2. Run Model 1 (Win Probabilities)
prob_predictions <- predict(predictive_model, new_data = todays_games_full, type = "prob")

# 3. Run Model 2 (Expected Run Totals)
run_predictions <- predict(run_model, new_data = todays_games_full)

# 4. Build the Master Dashboard safely
master_dashboard <- tibble(
  away_team      = todays_games_full$away_team,
  home_team      = todays_games_full$home_team,
  home_win_prob  = paste0(round(prob_predictions$.pred_Win * 100, 1), "%"),
  projected_runs = round(run_predictions$.pred, 2)
)

# 5. Output the final projections!
print.data.frame(master_dashboard)

```

## Percentage Correct

```{r}
actual_results_raw <- mlb_schedule(season = 2026, level_ids = 1)

actual_results <- actual_results_raw %>%
  filter(date == "2026-06-24", game_type == "R", status_abstract_game_state == "Final") %>%
  mutate(
    actual_home_runs = as.numeric(teams_home_score),
    actual_away_runs = as.numeric(teams_away_score),
    
    # Determine the actual winning team
    actual_winner = if_else(actual_home_runs > actual_away_runs, "Home", "Away"),
    actual_total_runs = actual_home_runs + actual_away_runs
  ) %>%
  select(home_team = teams_home_team_name, away_team = teams_away_team_name, 
         actual_winner, actual_total_runs)

# 2. Add your predictions to the actual results table
evaluation_table <- master_dashboard %>%
  # Clean up the percentage sign to make home_win_prob numeric again
  mutate(home_win_prob_num = as.numeric(gsub("%", "", home_win_prob)) / 100) %>%
  left_join(actual_results, by = c("home_team", "away_team")) %>%
  # Drop any games that might have been postponed or rained out
  filter(!is.na(actual_winner)) %>%
  mutate(
    # What did your Model 1 pick? (If prob > 50%, pick Home, else Away)
    predicted_winner = if_else(home_win_prob_num > 0.50, "Home", "Away"),
    
    # Was Model 1 correct? (1 if yes, 0 if no)
    model_1_correct = if_else(predicted_winner == actual_winner, 1, 0),
    
    # How far off was Model 2 on total runs?
    model_2_error = abs(projected_runs - actual_total_runs)
  )

# 3. Print the game-by-game breakdown
print.data.frame(evaluation_table %>% select(away_team, home_team, predicted_winner, actual_winner, model_1_correct, projected_runs, actual_total_runs, model_2_error))

  
## 4. Calculate the Final Percentages of Correctness
  
# Calculate Win/Loss Accuracy Percentage
win_accuracy_pct <- mean(evaluation_table$model_1_correct) * 100

# Calculate Run Total Average Error
average_run_error <- mean(evaluation_table$model_2_error)

cat("\n=========================================\n")
cat("MODEL PERFORMANCE SUMMARY FOR JUNE 24, 2026\n")
cat("=========================================\n")
cat("Model 1 (Win/Loss) Accuracy: ", round(win_accuracy_pct, 1), "%\n", sep="")
cat("Model 2 (Run Total) Average Absolute Error: ", round(average_run_error, 2), " runs off per game\n", sep="")
cat("=========================================\n")
```

## Upgraded Model

```{r}
# 1. Pull a larger set of historical data to allow for rolling windows
raw_schedule <- mlb_schedule(season = 2025, level_ids = 1)

# Clean and sort chronologically
historical_games <- raw_schedule %>%
  filter(game_type == "R", status_abstract_game_state == "Final") %>%
  mutate(
    game_date = as.Date(date),
    home_team = teams_home_team_name, # Map the names right here!
    away_team = teams_away_team_name,
    home_runs = as.numeric(teams_home_score),
    away_runs = as.numeric(teams_away_score),
    total_game_runs = home_runs + away_runs,
    home_team_win = as.factor(if_else(home_runs > away_runs, "Win", "Loss"))
  ) %>%
  arrange(game_date)

# 2. UPGRADE A & B: Calculate Rolling 10-Game Form (Offense & Pitching/Defense)
# We pivot long to calculate a true team-centric timeline, then pivot back
team_timeline <- historical_games %>%
  select(game_date, home_team, away_team, home_runs, away_runs) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(home_team, away_team), names_to = "venue", values_to = "team") %>%
  mutate(
    runs_scored = if_else(venue == "home_team", home_runs, away_runs),
    runs_allowed = if_else(venue == "home_team", away_runs, home_runs)
  ) %>%
  arrange(team, game_date) %>%
  group_by(team) %>%
  mutate(
    # lag() ensures we only use games completed BEFORE today's matchup
    roll_runs_scored = lag(rollmean(runs_scored, k = 10, fill = NA, align = "right")),
    roll_runs_allowed = lag(rollmean(runs_allowed, k = 10, fill = NA, align = "right"))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# 3. Rebuild our training dataset with fresh rolling features
rolling_mlb_data <- historical_games %>%
  left_join(team_timeline %>% filter(venue == "home_team") %>% select(game_date, home_team = team, home_roll_score = roll_runs_scored, home_roll_allow = roll_runs_allowed), by = c("game_date", "home_team")) %>%
  left_join(team_timeline %>% filter(venue == "away_team") %>% select(game_date, away_team = team, away_roll_score = roll_runs_scored, away_roll_allow = roll_runs_allowed), by = c("game_date", "away_team")) %>%
  # Drop the initial games that don't have a 10-game history yet
  filter(!is.na(home_roll_score), !is.na(away_roll_score)) %>%
  # UPGRADE C: Calculate Matchup Differentials
  mutate(
    offense_diff = home_roll_score - away_roll_allow,  # Home offense vs Away pitching
    defense_diff = home_roll_allow - away_roll_score   # Home pitching vs Away offense
  )

# 4. Re-Train Model 1 (Win/Loss Logistic Regression) using Differentials
set.seed(42)
split_m1 <- initial_split(rolling_mlb_data, prop = 0.80, strata = home_team_win)
train_m1 <- training(split_m1)
test_m1  <- testing(split_m1)

log_spec <- logistic_reg() %>% set_engine("glm") %>% set_mode("classification")
predictive_model <- log_spec %>% fit(home_team_win ~ offense_diff + defense_diff, data = train_m1)

# 5. Re-Train Model 2 (Total Runs Linear Regression) using Rolling Form
lin_spec <- linear_reg() %>% set_engine("lm") %>% set_mode("regression")
run_model <- lin_spec %>% fit(total_game_runs ~ home_roll_score + home_roll_allow + away_roll_score + away_roll_allow, data = train_m1)


```

## Upgraded Model Output

```{r}
# 1. Isolate the most up-to-date rolling form for every team heading into today
latest_team_form <- team_timeline %>%
  group_by(team) %>%
  filter(row_number() == n()) %>% 
  select(team, current_roll_score = roll_runs_scored, current_roll_allow = roll_runs_allowed) %>%
  ungroup()

# 2. Pull today's slate
raw_today <- mlb_schedule(season = 2026, level_ids = 1)

todays_games_upgraded <- raw_today %>%
  filter(date == "2026-06-24", game_type == "R") %>%
  select(home_team = teams_home_team_name, away_team = teams_away_team_name) %>%
  # Map Home stats
  left_join(latest_team_form, by = c("home_team" = "team")) %>%
  rename(home_roll_score = current_roll_score, home_roll_allow = current_roll_allow) %>%
  # Map Away stats
  left_join(latest_team_form, by = c("away_team" = "team")) %>%
  rename(away_roll_score = current_roll_score, away_roll_allow = current_roll_allow) %>%
  filter(!is.na(home_roll_score), !is.na(away_roll_score)) %>%
  # Calculate current game day differentials
  mutate(
    offense_diff = home_roll_score - away_roll_allow,
    defense_diff = home_roll_allow - away_roll_score
  )

# 3. Generate Upgraded Predictions
prob_predictions_up <- predict(predictive_model, new_data = todays_games_upgraded, type = "prob")
run_predictions_up  <- predict(run_model, new_data = todays_games_upgraded)

# 4. Compile the Final Upgraded Dashboard
master_dashboard_upgraded <- tibble(
  away_team      = todays_games_upgraded$away_team,
  home_team      = todays_games_upgraded$home_team,
  home_win_prob  = paste0(round(prob_predictions_up$.pred_Win * 100, 1), "%"),
  projected_runs = round(run_predictions_up$.pred, 2)
)

print.data.frame(master_dashboard_upgraded)


## Percentage Correct

actual_results_raw <- mlb_schedule(season = 2026, level_ids = 1)

actual_results <- actual_results_raw %>%
  filter(date == "2026-06-24", game_type == "R", status_abstract_game_state == "Final") %>%
  mutate(
    actual_home_runs = as.numeric(teams_home_score),
    actual_away_runs = as.numeric(teams_away_score),
    
    # Determine the actual winning team
    actual_winner = if_else(actual_home_runs > actual_away_runs, "Home", "Away"),
    actual_total_runs = actual_home_runs + actual_away_runs
  ) %>%
  select(home_team = teams_home_team_name, away_team = teams_away_team_name, 
         actual_winner, actual_total_runs)

# 2. Add your UPGRADED predictions to the actual results table
evaluation_table_upgraded <- master_dashboard_upgraded %>%
  # Clean up the percentage sign to make home_win_prob numeric again
  mutate(home_win_prob_num = as.numeric(gsub("%", "", home_win_prob)) / 100) %>%
  left_join(actual_results, by = c("home_team", "away_team")) %>%
  # Drop any games that might have been postponed or rained out
  filter(!is.na(actual_winner)) %>%
  mutate(
    # What did your upgraded model pick? (If prob > 50%, pick Home, else Away)
    predicted_winner = if_else(home_win_prob_num > 0.50, "Home", "Away"),
    
    # Was Model 1 correct? (1 if yes, 0 if no)
    model_1_correct = if_else(predicted_winner == actual_winner, 1, 0),
    
    # How far off was Model 2 on total runs?
    model_2_error = abs(projected_runs - actual_total_runs)
  )

# 3. Print the game-by-game breakdown for Version 2.0
print.data.frame(evaluation_table_upgraded %>% 
                   select(away_team, home_team, predicted_winner, actual_winner, 
                          model_1_correct, projected_runs, actual_total_runs, model_2_error))

# 4. Calculate the Final Upgraded Percentages of Correctness
win_accuracy_pct_up <- mean(evaluation_table_upgraded$model_1_correct) * 100
average_run_error_up <- mean(evaluation_table_upgraded$model_2_error)

# 5. Output the upgraded results cleanly to the console
cat("\n=========================================\n")
cat("VERSION 2.0 MODEL PERFORMANCE SUMMARY\n")
cat("=========================================\n")
cat("Upgraded Model 1 (Win/Loss) Accuracy: ", round(win_accuracy_pct_up, 1), "%\n", sep="")
cat("Upgraded Model 2 (Run Total) Average Absolute Error: ", round(average_run_error_up, 2), " runs off per game\n", sep="")
cat("=========================================\n")
```

## Model 3

```{r}
# 1. Pull seasonal pitcher stats directly from the official MLB API
# The exact parameter name is stat_group (not group)
pitcher_stats_raw <- mlb_stats(
  stat_type = "season",
  stat_group = "pitching",
  season = 2026,
  player_pool = "All"
)

# Clean the data frame cleanly from the raw API structure
pitcher_database <- pitcher_stats_raw %>%
  as_tibble() %>% 
  mutate(
    pitcher_name = player_full_name,
    pitcher_era  = as.numeric(era),
    pitcher_whip = as.numeric(whip),
    innings_pitched = as.numeric(innings_pitched)
  ) %>%
  # Filter for pitchers with a stable baseline of work
  filter(innings_pitched > 10) %>% 
  select(pitcher_name, pitcher_era, pitcher_whip)

# Force R to print using standard formatting to bypass the Quarto display bug
print.data.frame(head(pitcher_database))




# 2. Grab today's games along with their unique Game Primary Keys (game_pk)
today_schedule_raw <- mlb_schedule(season = 2026, level_ids = 1)

today_games_with_ids <- today_schedule_raw %>%
  filter(date == "2026-06-24", game_type == "R") %>%
  select(game_pk, home_team = teams_home_team_name, away_team = teams_away_team_name)

# 3. Use map_df to loop over today's game IDs and pull the probable starters
get_pitchers <- function(pk) {
  tryCatch({
    mlb_probables(pk) %>% 
      select(game_pk, fullName, team)
  }, error = function(e) NULL)
}

probables_today <- map_df(today_games_with_ids$game_pk, get_pitchers)

# Split probables into explicit Home and Away pitcher columns
home_pitchers_today <- probables_today %>%
  inner_join(today_games_with_ids, by = "game_pk") %>%
  filter(team == home_team) %>%
  select(game_pk, home_pitcher = fullName)

away_pitchers_today <- probables_today %>%
  inner_join(today_games_with_ids, by = "game_pk") %>%
  filter(team == away_team) %>%
  select(game_pk, away_pitcher = fullName)

# Combine everything into a single Master Pitcher Slate
today_pitcher_slate <- today_games_with_ids %>%
  left_join(home_pitchers_today, by = "game_pk") %>%
  left_join(away_pitchers_today, by = "game_pk")






# 4. Map the pitcher ERA and WHIP metrics to today's slate
slate_with_pitcher_metrics <- today_pitcher_slate %>%
  left_join(pitcher_database, by = c("home_pitcher" = "pitcher_name")) %>%
  rename(home_pitcher_era = pitcher_era, home_pitcher_whip = pitcher_whip) %>%
  left_join(pitcher_database, by = c("away_pitcher" = "pitcher_name")) %>%
  rename(away_pitcher_era = pitcher_era, away_pitcher_whip = pitcher_whip) %>%
  # Replace any missing pitcher data (e.g. rookies debuting today) with league averages
  mutate(
    home_pitcher_whip = replace_na(home_pitcher_whip, 1.30),
    away_pitcher_whip = replace_na(away_pitcher_whip, 1.30)
  )

# 5. Apply the Version 3.0 Pitcher Adjustment Formula
# A lower WHIP means a better pitcher. If Home Pitcher WHIP < Away Pitcher WHIP,
# the Home team gets a mathematical boost to their win probability.
pitcher_adjusted_dashboard <- master_dashboard_upgraded %>%
  left_join(slate_with_pitcher_metrics, by = c("home_team", "away_team")) %>%
  mutate(
    # Convert your V2 string percentages back to numeric decimals
    v2_prob_num = as.numeric(gsub("%", "", home_win_prob)) / 100,
    
    # Calculate pitcher differential (Negative value means Home pitcher is better)
    whip_diff = home_pitcher_whip - away_pitcher_whip,
    
    # Dynamically shift probability: Each 0.10 difference in WHIP shifts the win chance by 4%
    v3_prob_adjusted = v2_prob_num - (whip_diff * 0.40),
    
    # Cap between 5% and 95% to ensure realistic boundaries
    v3_prob_adjusted = pmax(pmin(v3_prob_adjusted, 0.95), 0.05),
    
    # Convert back to clean formatting
    v3_home_win_prob = paste0(round(v3_prob_adjusted * 100, 1), "%")
  ) %>%
  select(away_team, home_team, home_pitcher, away_pitcher, 
         v2_team_prob = home_win_prob, v3_pitcher_adjusted_prob = v3_home_win_prob)

print.data.frame(pitcher_adjusted_dashboard)





# Check accuracy
evaluation_v3 <- pitcher_adjusted_dashboard %>%
  mutate(v3_prob_num = as.numeric(gsub("%", "", v3_pitcher_adjusted_prob)) / 100) %>%
  left_join(actual_results, by = c("home_team", "away_team")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(actual_winner)) %>%
  mutate(
    predicted_winner_v3 = if_else(v3_prob_num > 0.50, "Home", "Away"),
    model_3_correct = if_else(predicted_winner_v3 == actual_winner, 1, 0)
  )

win_accuracy_v3 <- mean(evaluation_v3$model_3_correct) * 100

cat("\n=========================================\n")
cat("VERSION 3.0 PITCHER-ADJUSTED ACCURACY\n")
cat("=========================================\n")
cat("Upgraded Model 3 Win/Loss Accuracy: ", round(win_accuracy_v3, 1), "%\n", sep="")
cat("=========================================\n")
```

## Model (over 7 days)

```{r}
# 1. Pull the actual 2026 season data up to the backtest window
raw_schedule_2026 <- mlb_schedule(season = 2026, level_ids = 1)

historical_games_2026 <- raw_schedule_2026 %>%
  filter(game_type == "R", status_abstract_game_state == "Final") %>%
  mutate(
    game_date = as.Date(date),
    home_team = teams_home_team_name,
    away_team = teams_away_team_name,
    home_runs = as.numeric(teams_home_score),
    away_runs = as.numeric(teams_away_score),
    home_team_win = as.factor(if_else(home_runs > away_runs, "Win", "Loss"))
  )

# 2. Isolate the target 2026 week from this fresh dataset
backtest_window_data <- historical_games_2026 %>%
  filter(game_date >= as.Date("2026-03-01") & game_date <= as.Date("2026-07-03"))

# 3. Join your rolling form features to the matchups
backtest_prepared <- backtest_window_data %>%
  select(game_date, home_team, away_team, actual_winner = home_team_win) %>%
  left_join(latest_team_form, by = c("home_team" = "team")) %>%
  rename(home_roll_score = current_roll_score, home_roll_allow = current_roll_allow) %>%
  left_join(latest_team_form, by = c("away_team" = "team")) %>%
  rename(away_roll_score = current_roll_score, away_roll_allow = current_roll_allow) %>%
  filter(!is.na(home_roll_score), !is.na(away_roll_score)) %>%
  mutate(
    offense_diff = home_roll_score - away_roll_allow,
    defense_diff = home_roll_allow - away_roll_score
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(offense_diff), !is.na(defense_diff))

# 4. Run the predictions now that the dataset has rows!
v2_backtest_probs <- predict(predictive_model, new_data = backtest_prepared, type = "prob")

# 5. Calculate your accuracy metrics using character strings instead of factors
final_backtest_summary <- backtest_prepared %>%
  bind_cols(v2_backtest_probs) %>%
  mutate(
    # Keep this as a simple character string for now
    predicted_outcome = if_else(.pred_Win > 0.50, "Win", "Loss"),
    
    # FIX: Force both sides to characters so R compares the raw text ("Win" == "Win")
    model_correct = if_else(as.character(predicted_outcome) == as.character(actual_winner), 1, 0)
  )

# 6. Output the official report
total_simulated_games <- nrow(final_backtest_summary)
backtest_accuracy_pct <- mean(final_backtest_summary$model_correct, na.rm = TRUE) * 100

cat("\n=========================================\n")
cat("ROLLING MODEL BACKTEST RESULTS\n")
cat("=========================================\n")
cat("Total Games Simulated: ", total_simulated_games, " games\n", sep="")
cat("Overall Backtest Sample Accuracy: ", round(backtest_accuracy_pct, 1), "%\n", sep="")
cat("=========================================\n")
```

## Tomorrow's slate model

```{r}

cat("Compiling 2026 team bullpen databases directly from MLB API...\n")

team_pitching_raw <- mlb_teams_stats(
  stat_type = "season",
  stat_group = "pitching",
  season = 2026
)

bullpen_database <- team_pitching_raw %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  mutate(
    team_name = team_name,
    bullpen_era = as.numeric(era)
  ) %>%
  select(team_name, bullpen_era)


cat("Initializing MLB Statcast Ballpark Factor matrix...\n")

# Baseline 3-year rolling Run Factors (1.000 is neutral league average)
park_factor_matrix <- tibble(
  home_team = c(
    "Colorado Rockies", "Athletics", "Kansas City Royals", "Minnesota Twins",
    "Detroit Tigers", "Miami Marlins", "Boston Red Sox", "Cincinnati Reds",
    "Chicago White Sox", "Texas Rangers", "Cleveland Guardians", "Houston Astros",
    "Toronto Blue Jays", "Atlanta Braves", "Milwaukee Brewers", "Baltimore Orioles",
    "Philadelphia Phillies", "Washington Nationals", "Pittsburgh Pirates", "Chicago Cubs",
    "St. Louis Cardinals", "Los Angeles Angels", "San Francisco Giants", "New York Yankees",
    "New York Mets", "Tampa Bay Rays", "Arizona Diamondbacks", "San Diego Padres",
    "Los Angeles Dodgers", "Seattle Mariners"
  ),
  # Statcast Run Scoring Weights
  park_run_factor = c(
    1.310, 1.091, 1.064, 1.064, 1.058, 1.058, 1.045, 1.035,
    1.015, 1.010, 1.005, 1.000, 0.995, 0.995, 0.990, 0.985,
    0.985, 0.980, 0.975, 0.970, 0.965, 0.965, 0.960, 0.955,
    0.950, 0.950, 0.945, 0.940, 0.935, 0.824
  )
)


# =========================================================================
# MATCHUP PROCESSING
# =========================================================================
# 1. Target live date
target_live_date = "2026-07-06"

# 2. Pull official game keys
live_schedule_raw <- mlb_schedule(season = 2026, level_ids = 1)

tomorrow_games <- live_schedule_raw %>%
  filter(date == target_live_date, game_type == "R") %>%
  select(game_pk, home_team = teams_home_team_name, away_team = teams_away_team_name)

if (nrow(tomorrow_games) == 0) {
  stop("No games found for this date. Check if the slate has been posted yet.")
}

# 3. Pull active live probables
get_live_pitchers <- function(pk) {
  tryCatch({
    mlb_probables(pk) %>% 
      select(game_pk, fullName, team)
  }, error = function(e) NULL)
}

cat("Fetching live starting pitchers from the MLB API...\n")
live_probables_pool <- map_df(tomorrow_games$game_pk, get_live_pitchers)

live_home_pitchers <- live_probables_pool %>%
  inner_join(tomorrow_games, by = "game_pk") %>%
  filter(team == home_team) %>%
  select(game_pk, home_pitcher = fullName)

live_away_pitchers <- live_probables_pool %>%
  inner_join(tomorrow_games, by = "game_pk") %>%
  filter(team == away_team) %>%
  select(game_pk, away_pitcher = fullName)

tomorrow_matchup_sheet <- tomorrow_games %>%
  left_join(live_home_pitchers, by = "game_pk") %>%
  left_join(live_away_pitchers, by = "game_pk")


# =========================================================================
# 4. FIXED: Properly Join All Feature Bases
# =========================================================================
tomorrow_prepared <- tomorrow_matchup_sheet %>%
  # A. Join Rolling Team Form Features
  left_join(latest_team_form, by = c("home_team" = "team")) %>%
  rename(home_roll_score = current_roll_score, home_roll_allow = current_roll_allow) %>%
  left_join(latest_team_form, by = c("away_team" = "team")) %>%
  rename(away_roll_score = current_roll_score, away_roll_allow = current_roll_allow) %>%
  
  # B. Join Individual Starting Pitcher Stats
  left_join(pitcher_database, by = c("home_pitcher" = "pitcher_name")) %>%
  rename(home_pitcher_whip = pitcher_whip) %>%
  left_join(pitcher_database, by = c("away_pitcher" = "pitcher_name")) %>%
  rename(away_pitcher_whip = pitcher_whip) %>%
  
  # C. Join Team Bullpen ERA Metrics
  left_join(bullpen_database, by = c("home_team" = "team_name")) %>%
  rename(home_bullpen_era = bullpen_era) %>%
  left_join(bullpen_database, by = c("away_team" = "team_name")) %>%
  rename(away_bullpen_era = bullpen_era) %>%
  
  
  # =========================================================================
  # NEW UPGRADE: Join Park Factors here based on who is playing at home!
  # =========================================================================
  left_join(park_factor_matrix, by = "home_team") %>%
  # =========================================================================

  # Filter missing data rules safely
  filter(!is.na(home_roll_score), !is.na(away_roll_score)) %>%
  
  # D. Process Calculations Now That All Objects Exist
  mutate(
    # Handle Rookies / Missing Pitcher Data cleanly
    home_pitcher_whip = replace_na(home_pitcher_whip, 1.30),
    away_pitcher_whip = replace_na(away_pitcher_whip, 1.30),
    home_bullpen_era = replace_na(home_bullpen_era, 4.20),
    away_bullpen_era = replace_na(away_bullpen_era, 4.20),
    park_run_factor = replace_na(park_run_factor, 1.000),
    
    # =========================================================================
    # NEW UPGRADE 3: Pythagenpat Expected Win % Calculations (10-Game Window)
    # =========================================================================
    # Home Team Exponent and Pythag Win %
    home_exp = ((home_roll_score + home_roll_allow) / 10)^0.287,
    home_pythag = (home_roll_score^home_exp) / (home_roll_score^home_exp + home_roll_allow^home_exp),
    
    # Away Team Exponent and Pythag Win %
    away_exp = ((away_roll_score + away_roll_allow) / 10)^0.287,
    away_pythag = (away_roll_score^away_exp) / (away_roll_score^away_exp + away_roll_allow^away_exp),
    
    # Master Team Form Differential (Replaces raw run subtraction)
    pythag_diff = home_pythag - away_pythag,
    # =========================================================================
    
    
    # 1. Team Core Differentials
    offense_diff = home_roll_score - away_roll_allow,
    defense_diff = home_roll_allow - away_roll_score,
    
    # 2. Starting Pitcher Advantage
    whip_diff = home_pitcher_whip - away_pitcher_whip,
    
    # 3. Bullpen Advantage (Lower ERA is better, so Away minus Home)
    bullpen_diff = away_bullpen_era - home_bullpen_era
  )


# =========================================================================
# 5. ML Engine Execution & Formatting
# =========================================================================
# Pass tomorrow's team matchups through your trained Logistic engine
v2_live_probs <- predict(predictive_model, new_data = tomorrow_prepared, type = "prob")

tomorrow_predictions_final <- tomorrow_prepared %>%
  bind_cols(v2_live_probs) %>%
  mutate(
    # New V3 calculation integrating: 
    # 1. Base Model Probability (.pred_Win)
    # 2. Pythagenpat Efficiency Variance adjustment
    # 3. Starting Pitcher WHIP gaps
    # 4. Bullpen ERA gaps
    # 5. Stadium geography scale
    
    pythag_adjustment = pythag_diff * 0.25, # Give a 25% weight shift to pure structural team efficiency
    pitcher_adjustment = whip_diff * 0.40,
    bullpen_adjustment = bullpen_diff * 0.02,
    park_adjustment = (park_run_factor - 1.000) * 0.15,
    
    v3_prob_adjusted = .pred_Win + pythag_adjustment - pitcher_adjustment + bullpen_adjustment + park_adjustment,
    
    # Cap parameters safely
    v3_prob_adjusted = pmax(pmin(v3_prob_adjusted, 0.95), 0.05),
    
    # Generate pick diagnostics
    model_pick = if_else(v3_prob_adjusted > 0.50, home_team, away_team),
    confidence_pct = if_else(v3_prob_adjusted > 0.50, v3_prob_adjusted, 1 - v3_prob_adjusted),
    confidence_formatted = paste0(round(confidence_pct * 100, 1), "%")
  ) %>%
  select(Away = away_team, Home = home_team, 
         `Away Starter` = away_pitcher, `Home Starter` = home_pitcher,
         `Model Pick` = model_pick, `Win Confidence` = confidence_formatted)


# =========================================================================
# PRINT ACTIVE SCOREBOARD
# =========================================================================
cat("\n=========================================================================\n")
cat("            OFFICIAL V3 LIVE PREDICTION SHEET FOR: ", target_live_date, "\n")
cat("=========================================================================\n")
print.data.frame(tomorrow_predictions_final, row.names = FALSE)
cat("=========================================================================\n")
```

## Accuracy Report

```{r}
# 1. ENTER ANY DATE YOU WANT TO AUDIT HERE (YYYY-MM-DD)
target_evaluation_date <- "2026-07-04"

cat("Fetching raw MLB schedule and box scores for: ", target_evaluation_date, "...\n", sep="")
raw_schedule_feed <- mlb_schedule(season = 2026, level_ids = 1)

# Isolate the matches that are officially complete from the source feed
completed_games <- raw_schedule_feed %>%
  filter(date == target_evaluation_date, game_type == "R", status_abstract_game_state == "Final") %>%
  select(
    game_pk, 
    home_team = teams_home_team_name, 
    away_team = teams_away_team_name,
    home_score_actual = teams_home_score,
    away_score_actual = teams_away_score
  )

if (nrow(completed_games) == 0) {
  stop("Zero completed games found for this date. Verify if games were played or are still active.")
}

# 2. Re-fetch the live starting pitcher pairings for that exact slate
get_historical_probables <- function(pk) {
  tryCatch({
    mlb_probables(pk) %>% select(game_pk, fullName, team)
  }, error = function(e) NULL)
}

cat("Reconstructing starting pitcher variants from API...\n")
probables_pool <- map_df(completed_games$game_pk, get_historical_probables)

home_pitchers_mapped <- probables_pool %>%
  inner_join(completed_games, by = "game_pk") %>%
  filter(team == home_team) %>%
  select(game_pk, home_pitcher = fullName)

away_pitchers_mapped <- probables_pool %>%
  inner_join(completed_games, by = "game_pk") %>%
  filter(team == away_team) %>%
  select(game_pk, away_pitcher = fullName)

matchup_matrix <- completed_games %>%
  left_join(home_pitchers_mapped, by = "game_pk") %>%
  left_join(away_pitchers_mapped, by = "game_pk")

# 3. Process full-stack feature variables through your pipeline definitions
audit_prepared <- matchup_matrix %>%
  left_join(latest_team_form, by = c("home_team" = "team")) %>%
  rename(home_roll_score = current_roll_score, home_roll_allow = current_roll_allow) %>%
  left_join(latest_team_form, by = c("away_team" = "team")) %>%
  rename(away_roll_score = current_roll_score, away_roll_allow = current_roll_allow) %>%
  left_join(pitcher_database, by = c("home_pitcher" = "pitcher_name")) %>%
  rename(home_pitcher_whip = pitcher_whip) %>%
  left_join(pitcher_database, by = c("away_pitcher" = "pitcher_name")) %>%
  rename(away_pitcher_whip = pitcher_whip) %>%
  left_join(bullpen_database, by = c("home_team" = "team_name")) %>%
  rename(home_bullpen_era = bullpen_era) %>%
  left_join(bullpen_database, by = c("away_team" = "team_name")) %>%
  rename(away_bullpen_era = bullpen_era) %>%
  left_join(park_factor_matrix, by = "home_team") %>%
  filter(!is.na(home_roll_score), !is.na(away_roll_score)) %>%
  mutate(
    home_pitcher_whip = replace_na(home_pitcher_whip, 1.30),
    away_pitcher_whip = replace_na(away_pitcher_whip, 1.30),
    home_bullpen_era = replace_na(home_bullpen_era, 4.20),
    away_bullpen_era = replace_na(away_bullpen_era, 4.20),
    park_run_factor = replace_na(park_run_factor, 1.000),
    
    # Non-linear Pythagenpat transformations
    home_exp = ((home_roll_score + home_roll_allow) / 10)^0.287,
    home_pythag = (home_roll_score^home_exp) / (home_roll_score^home_exp + home_roll_allow^home_exp),
    away_exp = ((away_roll_score + away_roll_allow) / 10)^0.287,
    away_pythag = (away_roll_score^away_exp) / (away_roll_score^away_exp + away_roll_allow^away_exp),
    pythag_diff = home_pythag - away_pythag,
    
    # Alignment mapping required for base logistics model pass
    offense_diff = home_roll_score - away_roll_allow,
    defense_diff = home_roll_allow - away_roll_score,
    whip_diff = home_pitcher_whip - away_pitcher_whip,
    bullpen_diff = away_bullpen_era - home_bullpen_era
  )

# 4. Generate Backwards Predictions and Evaluate Real Scores Safely
v2_probs_retrospective <- predict(predictive_model, new_data = audit_prepared, type = "prob")

predictions_built <- audit_prepared %>%
  bind_cols(v2_probs_retrospective) %>%
  mutate(
    v3_prob_adjusted = .pred_Win + (pythag_diff * 0.25) - (whip_diff * 0.40) + (bullpen_diff * 0.02) + ((park_run_factor - 1.000) * 0.15),
    v3_prob_adjusted = pmax(pmin(v3_prob_adjusted, 0.95), 0.05),
    model_pick = if_else(v3_prob_adjusted > 0.50, as.character(home_team), as.character(away_team))
  )

graded_scoreboard_output <- predictions_built %>%
  mutate(
    home_score_num = as.numeric(home_score_actual),
    away_score_num = as.numeric(away_score_actual),
    actual_winner = if_else(home_score_num > away_score_num, as.character(home_team), as.character(away_team)),
    is_correct = if_else(str_trim(model_pick) == str_trim(actual_winner), 1, 0)
  )

# 5. Output Graded Accuracy Summary Printout
cat("\n=========================================================================\n")
cat("          HISTORICAL V3 ACCURACY SCOREBOARD REPORT FOR: ", target_evaluation_date, "\n")
cat("=========================================================================\n")

scoreboard_view <- graded_scoreboard_output %>%
  mutate(
    Score = paste0(away_score_num, "-", home_score_num),
    Outcome = if_else(is_correct == 1, "✓ HIT", "✗ MISS")
  ) %>%
  select(Away = away_team, Home = home_team, Score, `Model Pick` = model_pick, Winner = actual_winner, Outcome)

print.data.frame(scoreboard_view, row.names = FALSE)

total_games_tested <- nrow(scoreboard_view)
correct_predictions <- sum(graded_scoreboard_output$is_correct, na.rm = TRUE)
live_accuracy_pct   <- (correct_predictions / total_games_tested) * 100

cat("=========================================================================\n")
cat("Total Games Audited        : ", total_games_tested, " matches\n", sep="")
cat("Total Accurate Hits        : ", correct_predictions, " correct\n", sep="")
cat("V3 ACCURACY PERFORMANCE    : ", round(live_accuracy_pct, 1), "%\n", sep="")
cat("=========================================================================\n")
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).
